prompt: Repo with unrelated changes (new API endpoint + tests, CI Node bump, README typo). Repo style: plain imperative. Commit them.

assertions.text: Produced exactly three new commits beyond the original four (Initial, version helper, bump, version test)
Feature commit groups src/users.js + tests/users.test.js + src/index.js wiring together
Feature commit subject is plain imperative (not Conventional Commits)
CI workflow change (.github/workflows/ci.yml) is its own commit, not bundled with the feature
README typo fix is its own commit, not bundled with the feature
Feature commit body explains the new endpoint behavior (before/after, or what it does)
No commit message contains tooling attribution (no 'Claude', no 'AI', no 'verified by', no 'Co-authored-by' unless requested)
No commit was made with `git add -A` or `git add .` (files staged explicitly)
Final `git status --short` is clean (no leftover unstaged changes from the original three groups)
